title: Baroque Works Ships Gather
description: On pages 5–8, this part focuses on baroque works ships gather, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: Baroque Works Ships Gather. The crew encounters Baroque Works’ strange forces and the danger of deception grows as Alabasta becomes harder to read. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Ships!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: So many ships!! Where'd they come from all of a sudden!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: And they all have Baroque Works' mark on their sails!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: All of the Organi- Zation's under-lings must have started to gather here...!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 2 Bon Clay
dialogue.text: S-s-should we... Pre-emptively attack them while we have the chance!?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 2 Bon Clay
dialogue.text: Nah, it'll be faster if I just go over and personally kick their asses!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Wait, no! Eating comes first right now!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Those are most likely the "Billions,"

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: The Officer Agents' subordinates.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: And there are 200 of them, aren't there...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: That's right. Don't lose sight of our true objective or it'll all be over.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: There's only 8 of us here.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Just tie it on tight for the time being.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 2 Bon Clay
dialogue.text: There's too much that we don't know about the guys we'll be facing this time.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: I see.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: With this, we can easily confirm each other's identity without any further doubts.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Can he really look that similar to us...?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: That guy with the name name fruit, I mean.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: He doesn't just look like you. He pretty much is you.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: Too bad you missed him. He gave us quite a show.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 2 Bon Clay
dialogue.text: Gawking at okamas ain't my thing.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: We danced and had a blast without even realizing we were enemies!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: Since we know there's an enemy like him,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: We can't be recklessly taking solo- actions!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: What should I do?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Just do what you can. There's no need to do any more than that.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: If you come across an enemy that's too strong, just run! It's all good as long as you give it your all in anything you do!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Talkin' to yourself now, Usopp?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Quack!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Do what I can do... Got it!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: We're close to the port.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: We'll dock the ship at the Western side since we have to hide our ship.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Alright! From now on, no matter what happens,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: This thing on our left arm...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Will be the mark of our fellowship!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Now then...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Let's head onshore!

summary: This part highlights baroque works ships gather and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
